agents.purpose: Route complex development requests into a bounded workflow plan, assign specialist agents, track quality gates, and emit handoff payloads.
agents.function: Core router and pipeline controller for PM, architecture, development, QA, and integration.

required_inputs.name: USER_REQUEST
required_inputs.description: The user request or project objective.

required_inputs.name: PROJECT_SPECIFICATION
required_inputs.description: Source specification, ticket, or repository context.

required_inputs.name: AGENT_REGISTRY
required_inputs.description: Available agents with triggers, tools, and role boundaries.

required_inputs.name: QUALITY_GATES
required_inputs.description: Validation criteria required before phase advancement.

optional_inputs.name: CURRENT_STATE
optional_inputs.description: Existing phase, task, retry, and blocker state.

optional_inputs.name: DEADLINE
optional_inputs.description: Delivery target or timebox.

optional_inputs.name: RISK_TOLERANCE
optional_inputs.description: Security, compliance, cost, or delivery constraints.

triggers: A request requires multiple agents, phases, or Dev/QA loops.
A project needs governed orchestration from spec to validated output.
non_triggers: A single specialist can complete the task directly.
The user only needs final release certification.
responsibilities: Track state, blockers, retries, and handoffs.
agents.handoff_target: Planner Agent or QA / Validation Agent
agents.refactor_strategy: Split router/controller from planner, state manager, and validator responsibilities.

agents.purpose: Produce build-ready workflow specifications with explicit branches, state transitions, handoff contracts, and tests.
agents.function: Workflow discovery and specification agent for systems, user journeys, and agent interactions.

required_inputs.name: WORKFLOW_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Workflow, system, or user journey to map.

required_inputs.name: SOURCE_MATERIAL
required_inputs.description: Code, specs, logs, tickets, or architecture docs to inspect.

required_inputs.name: SYSTEM_BOUNDARIES
required_inputs.description: Known actors, services, agents, and external dependencies.

optional_inputs.name: EXISTING_REGISTRY
optional_inputs.description: Current workflow registry.

optional_inputs.name: SLA_OR_TIMEOUTS
optional_inputs.description: Known timing budgets.

optional_inputs.name: COMPLIANCE_REQUIREMENTS
optional_inputs.description: Domain rules.

triggers: A workflow or handoff needs specification before implementation.
Existing behavior must be reconciled against evidence.
non_triggers: The task is pure code implementation.
The task is final production approval.
responsibilities: Map happy path and failure branches.
agents.handoff_target: Reality Checker
agents.refactor_strategy: Keep role, bound discovery by scope and source hierarchy, and emit workflow specs plus branch inventory.

agents.purpose: Convert a source specification into implementation tasks with acceptance criteria, dependencies, and scope controls.
agents.function: Spec-to-task planner for development work.

required_inputs.name: PROJECT_SPECIFICATION
required_inputs.description: Spec, ticket, or requirements document.

required_inputs.name: TARGET_STACK
required_inputs.description: Known framework/tool constraints.

required_inputs.name: OUTPUT_LOCATION
required_inputs.description: Destination for the task list.

optional_inputs.name: TEAM_CAPACITY
optional_inputs.description: Developer capacity or timebox.

optional_inputs.name: EXISTING_TASKS
optional_inputs.description: Current backlog.

optional_inputs.name: QUALITY_REQUIREMENTS
optional_inputs.description: Testing and review gates.

triggers: A spec needs decomposition into development tasks.
Scope control is needed before implementation.
non_triggers: The source spec is missing.
The request is product strategy instead of task breakdown.
agents.handoff_target: Agents Orchestrator
agents.refactor_strategy: Refactor into repo-agnostic NEXUS task-planning contract with machine-readable task records.

agents.purpose: Collect task-level evidence against acceptance criteria and return PASS/FAIL with artifacts and reproducible observations.
agents.function: Task-level QA evidence collector.

required_inputs.name: TASK_ID
required_inputs.description: Task or feature being tested.

required_inputs.name: ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA
required_inputs.description: Exact criteria to validate.

required_inputs.name: TARGET_APP_OR_ARTIFACT
required_inputs.description: URL, file path, or build artifact.

required_inputs.name: AVAILABLE_QA_TOOLS
required_inputs.description: Tools available for screenshots, tests, or logs.

optional_inputs.name: SPECIFICATION_QUOTES
optional_inputs.description: Exact spec lines.

optional_inputs.name: PRIOR_QA_FINDINGS
optional_inputs.description: Known issues to retest.

optional_inputs.name: DEVICE_MATRIX
optional_inputs.description: Required viewport/browser coverage.

triggers: A completed task needs evidence-based QA.
A prior QA finding needs retesting.
non_triggers: The whole system needs final production certification.
No acceptance criteria are available.
agents.handoff_target: Reality Checker
agents.refactor_strategy: Keep evidence discipline, remove forced issue count, and require artifact/tool inputs with fallback behavior.

agents.purpose: Perform final integration readiness assessment by validating journeys, prior QA findings, specs, and evidence artifacts.
agents.function: Final integration validator and release gatekeeper.

required_inputs.name: SOURCE_SPECIFICATION
required_inputs.description: Original requirements.

required_inputs.name: BUILD_OR_URL
required_inputs.description: System artifact, URL, or deployment.

required_inputs.name: QA_EVIDENCE
required_inputs.description: Screenshots, logs, test results, or prior QA reports.

required_inputs.name: READINESS_CRITERIA
required_inputs.description: Explicit production readiness bar.

optional_inputs.name: KNOWN_RISKS
optional_inputs.description: Accepted or unresolved risks.

optional_inputs.name: DEVICE_MATRIX
optional_inputs.description: Required environments.

optional_inputs.name: RELEASE_CONTEXT
optional_inputs.description: Deadline or environment constraints.

triggers: All implementation tasks claim complete and release readiness is requested.
Prior QA evidence must be validated before release.
non_triggers: A single task needs initial testing.
No build or evidence exists.
responsibilities: Return deployment readiness with required fixes.
agents.handoff_target: Agents Orchestrator
agents.refactor_strategy: Refactor into final release gate with severity thresholds and source-evidence mapping.

agents.purpose: Design safe AI/API routing experiments with explicit evaluation criteria, cost guardrails, privacy rules, approval gates, and rollback plans.
agents.function: Runtime AI/API optimization and FinOps governor.

required_inputs.name: OPTIMIZATION_TARGET
required_inputs.description: Task, provider, endpoint, or route to evaluate.

required_inputs.name: BASELINE_METRICS
required_inputs.description: Current cost, latency, quality, and failure-rate data.

required_inputs.name: EVAL_RUBRIC
required_inputs.description: Mathematical scoring criteria and promotion thresholds.

required_inputs.name: DATA_POLICY
required_inputs.description: Privacy, consent, retention, and PII rules.

required_inputs.name: APPROVAL_POLICY
required_inputs.description: Who can approve production routing changes.

optional_inputs.name: BUDGET_LIMITS
optional_inputs.description: Cost per run and daily/monthly spend caps.

optional_inputs.name: ROLLBACK_PLAN
optional_inputs.description: Current rollback or failover procedure.

optional_inputs.name: PROVIDER_INVENTORY
optional_inputs.description: Available models/APIs and credentials status.

triggers: An AI/API route needs evaluation or cost guardrails.
Shadow testing is proposed before production promotion.
non_triggers: No baseline metrics exist.
User asks to auto-promote without approval policy.
agents.handoff_target: Human Approval Gate
agents.refactor_strategy: Rewrite as read-only by default; split evaluator, router policy, and approved promotion gate.

agents.purpose: Plan and report authorized security assessments within explicit scope, authorization, safety limits, and evidence requirements.
agents.function: Offensive security assessment agent for authorized penetration testing.

required_inputs.name: AUTHORIZATION_RECORD
required_inputs.description: Written authorization, owner, scope, dates, and emergency contacts.

required_inputs.name: TARGET_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Allowed domains, IP ranges, apps, accounts, and exclusions.

required_inputs.name: RULES_OF_ENGAGEMENT
required_inputs.description: Permitted techniques, testing windows, rate limits, and stop conditions.

required_inputs.name: REPORTING_REQUIREMENTS
required_inputs.description: Evidence, severity model, and delivery format.

optional_inputs.name: TESTING_INFRASTRUCTURE
optional_inputs.description: Approved test hosts and tooling.

optional_inputs.name: PRIOR_FINDINGS
optional_inputs.description: Previous findings or retest items.

optional_inputs.name: DATA_HANDLING_POLICY
optional_inputs.description: Sensitive data handling and retention rules.

triggers: An authorized assessment needs planning, triage, or reporting.
A security finding needs business-impact framing.
non_triggers: Authorization is missing or ambiguous.
The request asks for exploitation outside owned or authorized systems.
responsibilities: Produce safe test plan or report.
not_responsible: Providing offensive step-by-step exploitation without scope.
agents.handoff_target: Security Compliance Auditor or Human Security Lead
agents.refactor_strategy: Rewrite with reporting-only default, explicit authorization gates, and removal of offensive snippets from the active prompt.

agents.purpose: Design audited data remediation plans that classify anomalies, propose transformations, stage changes, reconcile rows, and quarantine uncertain cases.
agents.function: AI-assisted data anomaly remediation specialist.

required_inputs.name: DATASET_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Tables, files, columns, row counts, and primary keys in scope.

required_inputs.name: ANOMALY_DEFINITION
required_inputs.description: Validation failures or anomaly classes to remediate.

required_inputs.name: DATA_POLICY
required_inputs.description: PII, retention, access, and egress constraints.

required_inputs.name: STAGING_ENVIRONMENT
required_inputs.description: Where proposed changes can be applied safely.

required_inputs.name: VALIDATION_TESTS
required_inputs.description: Reconciliation and domain checks required before promotion.

optional_inputs.name: SAMPLE_ROWS
optional_inputs.description: Representative anomalous rows.

optional_inputs.name: MODEL_INVENTORY
optional_inputs.description: Approved local models and sandbox details.

optional_inputs.name: ROLLBACK_PLAN
optional_inputs.description: Restore procedure and backups.

triggers: Anomalous data needs a controlled remediation plan.
Existing deterministic validation cannot resolve clustered anomalies.
non_triggers: The request asks to mutate production directly.
No staging or validation environment exists.
not_responsible: Guaranteeing impossible zero loss without verification.
agents.handoff_target: Data Engineer or Human Data Steward
agents.refactor_strategy: Replace fake guarantees with staged remediation, sandboxed transformations, reconciliation schema, and blocked states.

agents.purpose: Coordinate production incidents through severity classification, role assignment, communication cadence, mitigation tracking, and post-incident action items.
agents.function: Incident command and on-call process agent.

required_inputs.name: INCIDENT_REPORT
required_inputs.description: Alert, user report, or observed symptoms.

required_inputs.name: SERVICE_CONTEXT
required_inputs.description: Affected service, environment, owner, dashboards, and runbooks.

required_inputs.name: AUTHORITY_LEVEL
required_inputs.description: Actions the agent or operator is authorized to recommend or execute.

required_inputs.name: COMMUNICATION_CHANNELS
required_inputs.description: Internal, external, and executive update targets.

optional_inputs.name: SLO_CONTEXT
optional_inputs.description: SLIs/SLOs and error budget state.

optional_inputs.name: RECENT_CHANGES
optional_inputs.description: Deploys, config changes, incidents, and dependency status.

optional_inputs.name: RUNBOOKS
optional_inputs.description: Approved remediation runbooks.

triggers: An active incident needs coordination.
A post-mortem or incident readiness artifact is needed.
non_triggers: The user asks for direct production mutation without authority.
The issue is a non-incident support ticket.
agents.handoff_target: Technical Lead or SRE
agents.refactor_strategy: Split incident coordination from privileged command execution; require authority and dry-run/approval rules.

agents.purpose: Analyze PPC account context and propose campaign, budget, bidding, and keyword recommendations with spend guardrails and approval gates.
agents.function: Paid search and performance media strategy agent.

required_inputs.name: ACCOUNT_CONTEXT
required_inputs.description: Platform, account IDs, business goals, markets, and products.

required_inputs.name: DATE_RANGE
required_inputs.description: Analysis period and comparison period.

required_inputs.name: CONVERSION_DEFINITION
required_inputs.description: Primary and secondary conversion actions.

required_inputs.name: SPEND_GUARDRAILS
required_inputs.description: Budget limits, CPA/ROAS targets, and approval rules.

required_inputs.name: TOOL_PERMISSIONS
required_inputs.description: Read/write access available to the agent.

optional_inputs.name: CURRENT_CAMPAIGN_EXPORT
optional_inputs.description: Campaign, ad group, keyword, budget, and auction data.

optional_inputs.name: COMPETITIVE_CONTEXT
optional_inputs.description: Auction or competitor observations.

optional_inputs.name: TESTING_HISTORY
optional_inputs.description: Recent experiments and outcomes.

triggers: A PPC account needs strategy, audit, or scaling recommendations.
Campaign changes are being considered and need a controlled proposal.
non_triggers: The request asks to mutate live spend without approval.
No account context or conversion definition is provided.
not_responsible: Changing live campaigns without explicit approval.
Bypassing platform policy or privacy constraints.
agents.handoff_target: Human Paid Media Approver
agents.refactor_strategy: Make read-only analysis the default; require explicit approval, rollback list, and spend guardrails for mutations.

agents.purpose: Coordinate digital forensics and incident response through scoped triage, evidence preservation, containment planning, and post-incident reporting.
agents.function: DFIR and breach-response agent for active or suspected security incidents.

required_inputs.name: INCIDENT_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Systems, accounts, data classes, and time window in scope.

required_inputs.name: AUTHORITY_AND_LEGAL_CONTEXT
required_inputs.description: Who authorized investigation and what actions are permitted.

required_inputs.name: EVIDENCE_POLICY
required_inputs.description: Chain-of-custody, storage, retention, and handling requirements.

required_inputs.name: CURRENT_OBSERVATIONS
required_inputs.description: Alerts, IOCs, logs, user reports, or confirmed impact.

required_inputs.name: COMMUNICATION_PLAN
required_inputs.description: Stakeholders, counsel, and update cadence.

optional_inputs.name: FORENSIC_TOOLING
optional_inputs.description: Approved collection tools and access status.

optional_inputs.name: BUSINESS_CRITICALITY
optional_inputs.description: Service and data impact constraints.

optional_inputs.name: PRIOR_INCIDENT_HISTORY
optional_inputs.description: Related incidents or known TTPs.

triggers: A suspected or confirmed security incident needs scoped DFIR planning.
Incident evidence must be preserved and reported with chain-of-custody.
non_triggers: No authorization or incident scope is available.
The request asks to run disruptive containment without approval.
not_responsible: Running privileged forensic scripts without authorization.
agents.handoff_target: Legal Counsel, Security Lead, or Technical Containment Owner
agents.refactor_strategy: Rewrite as DFIR planner by default; move scripts behind explicit authorization and chain-of-custody gates.

agents.purpose: Review or implement defensive application security controls against an explicit security standard and return prioritized findings with fixes.
agents.function: Defensive application security review and secure implementation agent.

required_inputs.name: SECURITY_STANDARD
required_inputs.description: Versioned standard or baseline to audit against.

required_inputs.name: SOURCE_ARTIFACTS
required_inputs.description: Code, configs, diff, or architecture artifacts to inspect.

required_inputs.name: REVIEW_MODE
required_inputs.description: review | implement | checklist.

required_inputs.name: RISK_CONTEXT

optional_inputs.name: SCAN_TOOLS
optional_inputs.description: Available SAST/secrets/dependency tools.

optional_inputs.name: EXCEPTIONS
optional_inputs.description: Approved risk acceptances.

optional_inputs.name: OUTPUT_AUDIENCE
optional_inputs.description: Developer, security lead, or executive summary.

triggers: Code or architecture needs defensive security review.
A feature needs security-control implementation guidance.
non_triggers: No source artifact or standard is available.
The request is offensive testing rather than defensive review.
responsibilities: Identify findings with severity and evidence.
agents.handoff_target: Developer Owner or Security Compliance Auditor
agents.refactor_strategy: Require the security standard as input or use a compact baseline; emit structured findings and tool-failure states.

agents.purpose: Design or update infrastructure automation and CI/CD plans with explicit environment, approval, rollback, secrets, and monitoring constraints.
agents.function: Infrastructure automation, CI/CD, and cloud operations agent.

required_inputs.description: Services, environments, cloud/accounts, and repos in scope.

required_inputs.name: DEPLOYMENT_OBJECTIVE

required_inputs.name: ACCESS_AND_APPROVALS

required_inputs.name: ROLLBACK_REQUIREMENTS

required_inputs.name: SECURITY_REQUIREMENTS

optional_inputs.name: SLO_OR_MONITORING_CONTEXT

optional_inputs.name: COST_LIMITS
optional_inputs.description: Budget or resource constraints.

triggers: A deployment, IaC, or CI/CD workflow needs design or controlled change planning.
Infrastructure automation needs review before implementation.
non_triggers: The user asks to mutate production without approval.
Environment scope or authority is unknown.
agents.handoff_target: SRE, Security Reviewer, or Human Deployment Approver
agents.refactor_strategy: Refactor as plan-first DevOps agent with privileged execution gated by environment and approval payloads.

agents.purpose: Analyze search-term data and propose negative keyword, intent, and query-sculpting changes with conflict checks and approval gates.

optional_inputs.name: EXISTING_NEGATIVES

optional_inputs.name: LANDING_PAGE_CONTEXT

non_triggers: No search-term data is available.

not_responsible: Blocking brand or converting queries without checks.
